summary: Install-time wallet and credential stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded IP
details: The package's postinstall.js (also declared as preinstall and main in package.json) runs unconditionally on npm install and scans the installer's home, Desktop, Documents, Downloads, /root, and /tmp directories for crypto wallet artifacts (metamask.json, phantom.json, keystore.json, wallet.dat, id.json, seed/mnemonic files, private key files) and developer credentials (.env, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.ssh private keys, Ethereum keystores, and Chrome/Brave/Edge MetaMask and Phantom extension storage). Matching file contents (up to 10KB per file) are POSTed as JSON — along with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and cwd — over plaintext HTTP to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package name impersonates a Binance SDK but ships no SDK functionality (empty description, main points directly at the stealer script) — a lure targeting crypto developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
